title: Ricey Fluffy Saudi-Arabian Rice Pilaf
description: A tangy, fluffy, cozy Saudi-Arabian dish made with lemon juice and a sweet glaze, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This dish is also Jain-friendly.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of ghee or oil in a donabe over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until it turns translucent.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute.
Add the basmati rice and stir to coat the rice with the oil and mix with the onion and garlic.
Add 2 cups of water and salt to taste. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the donabe, and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
While the rice is cooking, prepare the glaze by mixing together the lemon juice, sugar, and rose water (if using).
Once the rice is cooked, fluff it with a fork and add the glaze, stirring to combine.
Cook over low heat for another 5 minutes, stirring constantly, until the rice is coated with the glaze and heated through.
Serve hot and garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
